column1: # Owner: Jane. Machine-readable. John: parse this, don't hardcode weapon numbers.
# Tab-separated. '#' comments and blank lines ignored.
# One row per (weapon, level). Values are ABSOLUTE, not deltas — no formula for
# you to reimplement, and I can hand-tune any single cell.
# ALL DISTANCES ARE WORLD UNITS. design.md §5: 1 cell = 1 wu wide x 2 wu tall.
# A band 6 wu tall renders as 3 rows. A ring of radius r draws as an ellipse
# bolt     hit radius        hit radius      wu/s, homing
# arc      burst radius      burst radius    wu/s, lobbed
# orbit    orbit radius      hit radius      degrees/s
# cd=0 means "always on" (orbit). pierce 99 = effectively infinite.
# dur = lifetime of the effect in seconds (bolts, embers, columns).
# count = simultaneous instances (bolts fired, orbiting motes, columns).
# LEVEL 9 IS THE EVOLVED FORM. Each weapon has exactly one evolution, so no new
# schema is needed. Level-up cards must NEVER offer level 9 -- it is granted only
# by opening a chest while the weapon is at 8 and its paired passive is owned.
# Evolving is a whole tier, not a level: dmg x1.70, cooldown x0.75, area x1.30,
# infinite pierce, plus the clause. It has to be FELT.
# COLOURS obey design.md §9: bright white is the player's, bright cyan is XP's,
# bright yellow is reward's, bright green is healing's. A weapon may not take one.
# ###  THE `note` COLUMN IS PLAYER-FACING COPY. IT IS PRINTED ON THE LEVEL-UP CARD.  ###
# ###  BUDGET: 36 CHARACTERS, AND IT MUST WORD-WRAP INTO TWO LINES OF TWENTY.        ###
# upgrades.ts prints `note` verbatim as the card's effect line. It is not a
# comment field. Until 10.07 it held our engineering shorthand, so a player
#     "ax = orbit radius, ay = hit radius, pspeed = deg/s"
# and a player offered The Chain read "no longer the starting weapon."
# THE BUDGET, added later the same day. The card is 24 columns wide (MIN_COLS is 80,
# and 3x24 + 2x3 = 78), so app.ts:504 hands the sentence 20 columns -- and TRUNCATED.
# 17 of these 28 notes were cut, including every level-1 introduction, the one line
# whose whole job is to explain a weapon the player has never seen:
#     "Fires a seeking bolt at the nearest enemy."  reached the player as
# 36 chars is a gift, not a tax. That note is now "A bolt seeks the nearest enemy."
# and it is better copy. Forty characters was never a sentence anyone reads in two
# seconds; it was prose in a spreadsheet cell because nothing pushed back.
# Write it as one short sentence, for the player, in the game's voice.
# Leave it BLANK on a level that only moves numbers -- the card then falls back
# to "17 damage · 1.02s cooldown", which is exactly what the player wants there.
# Engineering commentary goes in `#` lines like this one, where nobody can ship it.

column16: Sweeps a wide band to either side.
Adds a second band. Now a cross.
The circle closes. It never misses.
A bolt seeks the nearest enemy.
Each bolt chains to four targets.
A burning ring turns around you.
The ring sets the floor alight.
Lobs salt that shatters on impact.

column1: # orbits you." — a continuity break post-pivot (no physical lantern exists
# anymore; the player is a ship). `id` stays `lantern` — matches
# characters.tsv's `start_weapon` column and evolutions.tsv, not worth the
# coordination cost of renaming an id both agents and two other tables read.

column16: Moonlight falls in columns near you.
One column, the height of the sky.
Fire spreads from ember to ember.
